0024c0284321b7e1.summary: Discover the MOTAT collection like never before during this unique behind-the-scenes tour.
0024c0284321b7e1.description: This is a unique opportunity to explore the MOTAT collection from behind the scenes. Attendees will get an exclusive look at the museum's vast holdings, discovering items and areas usually kept private from the general public. Don't miss these special extra dates for an unparalleled deep dive into the collection.

021f448b54049758.summary: Hear Dr Josh Emmitt discuss the daily journey of discovery, from excavating new information to challenging misconceptions in archaeology.
021f448b54049758.description: Digging into the past, excavating new information and challenging misconceptions is all in a day's work of a museum archaeologist. Dr Josh Emmitt, Curator Archaeology at Auckland Museum, takes attendees through his daily journey of discovery during this special talk for New Zealand Archaeology Week.

034e0f1e13de5956.summary: Commemorate ANZAC Day with dawn service, tours, performances.
034e0f1e13de5956.description: This Anzac Day, Auckland Museum hosts a day of commemoration featuring the Dawn Service, free guided tours, choral performances, and poetry readings. Entry to the museum is complimentary for all visitors on this day. The museum opens immediately after the Dawn Service.

0625a85ce3dcc2fd.summary: A captivating journey through time uncovering the secrets behind the iconic Clock Tower.
0625a85ce3dcc2fd.description: This talk unveils the history and secrets of the iconic Clock Tower. Attendees will gain insights into the architectural significance and century-long story of this prominent local landmark. Discover the enduring narrative embedded within this Auckland fixture.

07ddca6f970286a6.summary: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, exploring movement for wellbeing, honouring land, ocean, and community.
07ddca6f970286a6.description: Participants explore foundational Siva Samoa steps and their cultural meanings—gestures that honour connections to land, ocean, sky, ancestry, and community. Through soft hands, grounded feet, and mindful breath, attendees experience how movement mirrors the natural world. Rooted in traditional practice, the session invites people to slow down, reconnect, and honour the body. Open to all levels.

0b472d46885f049a.summary: The lecture explores making sure that embedded technology in daily life remains reliable, usable, and safe.
0b472d46885f049a.description: Technology integrates into virtually every aspect of modern existence. This discussion addresses critical questions regarding the reliability, usability, and safety of these embedded technologies in our daily lives. The audience will gain insight into the challenges inherent in maintaining trust and security within pervasive digital systems.

0bdfb3b4492e9d85.summary: Get ready to turn up the volume! Sounds of Tāmaki Makaurau is coming this NZ Music Month 2026!
0bdfb3b4492e9d85.description: Get ready to turn up the volume! Sounds of Tāmaki Makaurau is coming this NZ Music Month 2026! This region-wide celebration will spotlight music-themed events and experiences, brought to life by Auckland Council. From community stages to creative experiences, the month will shine a light on local talent, including a special emerging artist series. Keep your ears open, exciting new voices are set to step into the spotlight at Studio One Toi Tū.

0c5a2aaa12aaa6f5.summary: Professor John Dudley will give an overview of photonics, detailing the interplay between basic science and innovation.
0c5a2aaa12aaa6f5.description: This talk provides an overview of photonics, exploring this enabling technology. It will detail the critical interplay that exists between fundamental basic science research and practical innovation within the field. Join us at the University of Auckland for this insightful presentation.

0ce3b2985b99250e.summary: Dr Deane Galbraith examines Io as ‘the Soul of Things,’ exploring Māori Io traditions beyond the Supreme God debate.
0ce3b2985b99250e.description: Dr Deane Galbraith (Ngāpuhi, Te Rarawa), a Senior Lecturer in Religion/Mātai Whairuataka at the University of Otago/Ōtākou Whakaihu WakaFew, debates current understandings of Io.

speakers.name: Dr Deane Galbraith
speakers.bio: Senior Lecturer in Religion/Mātai Whairuataka, University of Otago/Ōtākou Whakaihu WakaFew

0ce9fae1b4d99995.summary: Panel discussion on diaspora communities navigating global tensions.
0ce9fae1b4d99995.description: The NZIIA Wellington Branch Student Committee invites you to a panel discussion about the impact of rising global tensions on diaspora communities. Featuring Dr Kate McMillan and Dhilum Nightingale, this event will explore how geopolitical shifts affect migration, identity, and cross-border connections globally and in New Zealand.

0d0d2141e1bc8a11.summary: Tāmaki Makaurau musicians Ersha Island perform an intimate concert to celebrate the closing day of the exhibition Dreaming from afar.
0d0d2141e1bc8a11.description: Tāmaki Makaurau musicians Ersha Island will provide an intimate performance within the gallery, celebrating the closing day of the current exhibition, Dreaming from afar.

0f1ea4588f7224e7.summary: WEN Outreach Workshop on earthquake safety for young women.
0f1ea4588f7224e7.description: A free half-day workshop for Year 9 and 10 female students focusing on earthquake preparedness through creative problem-solving activities in engineering. Includes a complimentary pizza lunch.

1092d277ff445319.clean_title: Perspectives in data: Building Aotearoa's future with Databricks and Tech Women
1092d277ff445319.summary: Perspectives in data: Building Aotearoa's future with Databricks and Tech Women, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.
1092d277ff445319.description: This session addresses perspectives in data, focusing on building Aotearoa's future using Databricks and involving Tech Women, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.

110751ff6112b13a.description: Visit the Canterbury Museum Pop-Up for collection highlights and temporary exhibitions while our main building is redeveloped.

11dc8475abd89d26.summary: Tamariki Time workshop at Te Papa, making nature picture frames.
11dc8475abd89d26.description: Engage in creative activities at Tamariki Time: Slow Burn – Ahi Tāmau. Create your own nature picture frame using native plants from Bush City.

184d6aaa593d1460.summary: Professor Alexandra Andhov discusses 'Big Tech: Too Big To Fail?' in her inaugural lecture.
184d6aaa593d1460.description: Inaugural Professorial Lecture by Alexandra Andhov addressing the role and impact of big tech companies. The discussion delves into whether these entities are too large to fail, challenging the status quo of digital dominance.

189671d66f52d283.summary: Explore siapo-making as a living practice through talanoa.
189671d66f52d283.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, we will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.

19b443dab561d5b4.summary: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language Week with whānau-friendly activities on Rongomaraeroa, Te Papa’s marae.
19b443dab561d5b4.description: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language Week with whānau-friendly activities on Rongomaraeroa, Te Papa’s marae.
19b443dab561d5b4.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or talk component

1e4c2168b36632c7.summary: Celebrates the opening of Gus Fisher Gallery’s winter exhibitions programme featuring Claude Cahun, Marcel Moore, and Phone Tree.
1e4c2168b36632c7.description: Studies for a Keepsake marks the first exhibition of pioneering French Surrealists Claude Cahun (1894-1954) and Marcel Moore (1892-1972) in Aotearoa. Alongside this, Tāmaki Makaurau artist Lucy Meyle presents Phone Tree, exploring moth traps through drawing, casting, and lacework.

1e871946e014fdb7.summary: Celebrate the announcement of the 2026 Ockham New Zealand Book Awards longlist, featuring readings from authors in all four categories.

1f65330f90ae035b.summary: This panel explores the role of dyslexic thinking in modern business, leadership, and innovation, presenting it as a potential strategic advantage.
1f65330f90ae035b.description: The discussion examines how the cognitive patterns associated with dyslexia might translate into novel approaches for modern business challenges. Participants will explore how diverse thinking styles, including those often associated with dyslexia, can drive innovation and reshape leadership strategies. The session provides insights into harnessing this perspective for organizational advantage.

1f6536ed97b55103.summary: 2026 WSBG Climate Festival Fortnight at Victoria University
1f6536ed97b55103.description: Wellington School of Business and Government (WSBG) at Te Herenga Waka—Victoria University of Wellington supports Wellington City Council’s first climate festival. Register here for more information.

2006b7e3c699fb83.summary: Professor Jonathan Barrett presents on respecting equal human dignity through the lens of taxation and commercial law in the human rights state.
2006b7e3c699fb83.description: Professor Barrett's research concerns the practical application of human rights, particularly respecting equal human dignity through taxation and commercial law in the human rights state.

200da7812a57519d.summary: Lucy O’Hagan presents a reading and performance of her acclaimed memoir, Everything But the Medicine: A Doctor’s Tale.
200da7812a57519d.description: This presentation brings Lucy O’Hagan's acclaimed memoir, Everything But the Medicine: A Doctor’s Tale, to life through reading and performance. The memoir details experiences within the world of medicine.

212868d681362f71.summary: Explore Yongchun lacquer baskets and paint your own.
212868d681362f71.description: Discover the beauty of Yongchun Lacquer Baskets, a traditional craft from Fujian blending weaving and lacquer art. Learn about its cultural story and create your own lacquer basket under expert guidance.

21be6b77ec2e071a.summary: Examines Southeast Asia's critical juncture amidst world order fragmentation, considering the Iran War, Trump's tariffs, and the South China Sea.
21be6b77ec2e071a.description: World order fragments, leaving Southeast Asia at a critical juncture. The discussion will examine the interplay of the Iran War, President Trump’s sweeping tariff agenda, and tensions in the South China Sea.

22b6c643fdffc426.summary: Professor Papaarangi Reid leads a wānanga-style public discussion on persistent health inequities facing Māori communities and required system change.
22b6c643fdffc426.description: This discussion addresses the persistent health inequities confronting Māori communities. The conversation explores what genuine system change requires to improve Māori wellbeing across health systems. Attendees will gain insight into current progress and the systemic challenges that must be addressed for equitable health outcomes.

22f56ba953bbd60d.summary: Professor Jonathan Barrett presents his inaugural lecture on the practical application of human rights in taxation and commercial law within a human rights state.

speakers.name: Professor Jonathan Barrett
speakers.bio: Professor at Victoria University, focusing on the practical application of human rights.

2411337464843d25.summary: Explore Samoan female tattooing as cultural knowledge archive in a workshop by Grounded Rosanna.
2411337464843d25.description: This workshop centres the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence. Through discussion, drawing, writing, and embodied practice, participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, and how these markings intersect with gender, identity, and sovereignty. Grounded Rosanna's own malu and artistic practice inform the workshop's critical reflection on issues of cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems.

274567a260b0d69b.summary: Thailand, often referred to as “The Land of Smiles,” is known for its rich cultural heritage, striking temples, world-renowned cuisine, and welcoming communities.
274567a260b0d69b.description: Thailand, often referred to as “The Land of Smiles,” is known for its rich cultural heritage, striking temples, world-renowned cuisine, and welcoming communities. Learning Thai provides a meaningful way to experience this culture more deeply, whether for travel, personal interest, or future engagement with Thailand. Course Overview This introductory course is designed for complete beginners and those preparing to travel to Thailand.
274567a260b0d69b.exclude_reason: This is a language course, not a lecture.

27acf592ab1ec661.summary: Conference exploring critical reflections on development practices.
27acf592ab1ec661.description: This year’s theme, Rebalancing Worlds – Reworking Practice, invites us to reflect critically on development practice and explore pathways to more just and equitable futures in the field of international development. Organized by Victoria University's DEVNET program.

283045f10f790cd0.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 presents insights into the future of Open Insurance within Aotearoa New Zealand.
283045f10f790cd0.description: The session explores the trajectory and potential of Open Insurance within Aotearoa New Zealand. Hosted by Deloitte Techweek26, the event is held at Deloitte Wellington on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

287e6037f1b214e7.summary: This talk addresses the vital role of coral reefs, which support 25% of marine species and provide coastal defense and food sources.
287e6037f1b214e7.description: Following the 16th International Coral Reef Symposium hosted at Victoria University of Wellington, this presentation will cover the critical ecological, defensive, and economic importance of coral reefs.

29e57fcd6effffcd.summary: Queers & Wares is a market celebrating queer creativity, community, and connection. Browse unique handmade pieces, vintage clothing, and art from local artists.

2c5eaa40100d9760.summary: The Stout Centre’s seminar series continues with a discussion on decolonising the screen.
2c5eaa40100d9760.description: The Stout Centre’s seminar series continues with the forthcoming book on this topic. This event is presented by Leah Vonderheide.

2cb1e777f13f1b20.summary: The webinar explores rethinking the concept of 'normal' through the lens of the neurodivergent experience.
2cb1e777f13f1b20.description: This webinar examines contemporary ideas surrounding neurodiversity, challenging conventional understandings of what is considered 'normal.' Participants will gain insight into various perspectives on neurodivergent lives within a broader societal context. The discussion encourages a rethinking of normative standards across different aspects of life.

2cff4af0fa8b324e.summary: Professor Hella von Unger presents research in education.
2cff4af0fa8b324e.description: Join the School of Education for a seminar featuring visiting Professor Hella von Unger from Munich. Professor von Unger will discuss her latest research findings in educational studies. The event includes refreshments and networking opportunities.

2d964e637ef86463.summary: This screening presents 'Speaking in Stone,' a short film commissioned after the death of sculptor John Edgar ONZM, displayed within Martino Gamper’s exhibition Endgrained.
2d964e637ef86463.description: This screening presents 'Speaking in Stone,' a short film commissioned following the passing of sculptor John Edgar ONZM. Directed by Greg Wood with music by Peter Hobbs, the 19-minute film responds to the vast repository of John Edgar’s creative life found in his West Auckland studio. The film captures the essence of the artist using sounds and instruments created from tools and sounds recorded at the sculpture workshop.

2eea4335227e348d.summary: Dr Stephen Skalicky discusses the intersection of literal, figurative language, and Large Language Models.
2eea4335227e348d.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the. Presented by Dr Stephen Skalicky.

2ef8a4ebd6343108.summary: Mandarin Corner celebrates Mid-Autumn Festival with Chinese legends and traditions.
2ef8a4ebd6343108.description: Celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival and explore one of China’s most beloved traditions. Discover the legend of Chang’e, learn about mooncakes and festival customs. Hosted by Victoria University's Mandarin Corner.

2f6c979a89a98d02.summary: Shaun McRae and Frank Wolak will discuss how capacity mechanisms are used to ensure demand can be met in electricity markets dominated by renewables.
2f6c979a89a98d02.description: Capacity mechanisms are an increasingly popular tool for ensuring that demand can be met as electricity sources shift towards renewables. The discussion will explore the options available for maintaining system reliability in these new market structures.

310f8cfdd19d4d16.summary: This webinar explores themes of generative AI, contaminated information, and rhubarb in the 'This Thinking Life' series.
310f8cfdd19d4d16.description: This session tackles the complex interplay between rapidly evolving generative AI, the challenges posed by contaminated information, and the seemingly mundane element of rhubarb. The discussion examines modern themes through a critical, interdisciplinary lens.

3119f8036c8cc821.summary: A panel discussion exploring aerial mapping technology held at the MOTAT Aviation Hall.
3119f8036c8cc821.description: Join MOTAT for an evening panel discussion in the Aviation Hall. Attendees will explore the latest developments and applications within aerial mapping technology. This promises an engaging conversation for aviation enthusiasts and industry professionals alike.

3139979a01b04c3d.summary: Participants will explore the history and cultural significance of siapo-making in Samoa through a two-day workshop format.
3139979a01b04c3d.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, the session will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance, and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.

33001181c2a3a6b3.summary: Vice-Chancellor Damon Salesa discusses AI and Data Sovereignty with industry experts, focusing on the critical need for trust and ethics.
33001181c2a3a6b3.description: Vice-Chancellor Damon Salesa will discuss AI and Data Sovereignty with a panel of industry experts. The discussion will explore the critical need for trust and ethics surrounding these technologies.

347ac28eebc4b22c.summary: Dancer Xin Ji and musician Alistair Deverick present a live performance within Martino Gamper’s exhibition, merging movement, sound, and real-time composition.
347ac28eebc4b22c.description: Xin Ji and Alistair Deverick stage a live performance within Martino Gamper’s exhibition Endgrained, bringing together movement, sound, and real-time composition around Gamper’s furniture. Drawing on ideas from Xin’s solo work Body Story, the performance alternates between dance and live music, letting meaning emerge through presence, texture, and exchange. The work is shaped by physicality, rhythm, improvisation, and close attention to what unfolds in the room.

3755a9be782fce30.summary: Victoria's Mandarin Corner explores Tai Chi basics.
3755a9be782fce30.description: Victoria University hosts a Mandarin Corner session focused on Tai Chi, inviting participants to practice this traditional Chinese martial art. The event combines language immersion with cultural experience through guided Tai Chi movements.

39f8376b33a79ab2.summary: Archivists discuss new discoveries at the Alexander Turnbull Library.
39f8376b33a79ab2.description: Archivists and historians unveil recently catalogued items from the Alexander Turnbull Library collection, featuring rare photographs and correspondence. These findings shed light on New Zealand's past and contribute to historical research.

3aba32013f4fbb71.description: Fast Forward 2026: Transcolonisation! Semester 1 In the Architecture NZ article Transcolonisation 1990-2020 , Anthony Hōete charted how architecture in Aotearoa was negotiating the complex entanglement of colonial inheritance and Indigenous resurgence. He described a 30-year trajectory in which design culture shifts from bicultural acknowledgement toward a more assertive Te Ao Māori architectural project, seeking to reconcile European traditions with Indigenous spatial knowledge.

3dff430382117182.summary: This webinar kicks off the 'This Thinking Life' series, focusing on the critical intersection of Indigeneity and Artificial Intelligence.
3dff430382117182.description: The 'This Thinking Life' series begins with an examination of how Artificial Intelligence interacts with Indigenous knowledge systems. Participants will explore the complex ethical and technological implications at this frontier.

40f85f794ad4a481.summary: This lecture explores what happens when disability is viewed as a powerful cultural lens rather than merely a problem to solve.
40f85f794ad4a481.description: What happens when disability is seen as a powerful cultural lens, not a problem to solve? Join fellow AUT graduates for this insightful Great Graduates Speaker Series event.

458a181125ff16de.summary: Celebrate the book launch for Oceanic Architectural Routes: The Work of Mike Austin, featuring contributions from Austin's former students and colleagues.
458a181125ff16de.description: The book launch celebrates Oceanic Architectural Routes: The Work of Mike Austin, bringing together contributions from Austin's former students and colleagues. The book is designed by Shaun Naufahu and features photographs from Austin's travels across the Oceanic region. Attendees will hear from Albert L. Refiti, Icao Tiseli, and Mike, alongside music from SANGUINE.

47b8c89e63f1e5a3.summary: A creative workshop using printmaking and mindfulness to explore the familiar paths that bring a sense of comfort and connection to our daily routines.

47bff4f0c2afd6c2.summary: The third webinar in the 'This Thinking Life' series explores the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and human communication.
47bff4f0c2afd6c2.description: This session examines the rapidly changing landscape where Artificial Intelligence intersects with human communication methods. Attendees will gain insights into the implications of AI on how we connect, share information, and construct meaning in the modern digital environment.

481fca738bd99f86.summary: Mark the closing of the exhibition He Kaupapa Waka with an afternoon of talks.
481fca738bd99f86.description: The closing of the exhibition He Kaupapa Waka features an afternoon of talks marking the end of the display. This gathering offers attendees a rare opportunity to connect with the themes explored in the exhibition through discussion. The event serves as a concluding reflection on the cultural and historical significance of the waka.

4dfb6447940ce651.summary: This talk examines Asia-Pacific LNG trends to understand how New Zealand can maintain energy security and stability.
4dfb6447940ce651.description: The energy sector faces complex challenges regarding reliable and sustainable supply. This discussion analyzes the recent trends in Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) across the Asia-Pacific region. Attendees will gain insight into how international energy dynamics can inform and strengthen New Zealand's own national energy balancing strategies.

4eb49528e01bc8dd.summary: This symposium will bring together researchers to discuss cutting-edge work in cosmology, particle astrophysics, and general relativity, covering topics like dark matter and gravitational waves.
4eb49528e01bc8dd.description: This symposium integrates the International Symposium on Cosmology and Particle Astrophysics (CosPA2026) and the Australasian Conference on General Relativity and Gravitation (ACGRG13v). The event aims to convene theorists, experimentalists, and observers to discuss cutting-edge observations and theoretical developments across cosmology, particle physics, and gravity. Key areas of discussion include classical and quantum theories of gravity, dark matter, and gravitational waves.

52cbd11960f48258.summary: Shaun Higgins discusses WWII POW art.
52cbd11960f48258.description: Curator Shaun Higgins explores how an Australian POW in WWII created remarkable portraits within a prison camp. Learn about the ingenuity and resilience of these artists during wartime adversity.

541dc5ec63ddc292.summary: Clinton Duffy, Curator of Marine Biology, guides attendees through the marine collections before the Sharks exhibition travels overseas, allowing encounters with specimens like the Hammerhead Shark.
541dc5ec63ddc292.description: Join Clinton Duffy, Curator of Marine Biology, for a guided talk into the world of sharks and the marine collections. This session offers a chance to meet unique specimens, including the Hammerhead Shark, before the Sharks exhibition departs for overseas.

543b9068d04b75b5.summary: The Weaponisation of Economics and How we Might Respond: The Art of Economic
543b9068d04b75b5.description: The Weaponisation of Economics and How we Might Respond: The Art of Economic

5506b567e7f48ff1.summary: Explore ideas to create a high-performing health system for New Zealand, featuring insights from world health policy expert Dr Ezekiel Emanuel.
5506b567e7f48ff1.description: Drawing from extensive comparative study of 11 nations, this summit will move beyond simplistic rankings to identify and discuss the core components of world-class health systems. The event features keynotes from Dr Ezekiel Emanuel, a world-renowned health policy expert and author of Which Country Has The World’s Best Health Care?

575e90640f923cd8.summary: Discover how science and creativity coincide through a hands-on cyanotype workshop using natural materials.
575e90640f923cd8.description: Experiment with exposure, shadow, and UV light to create your own unique print using natural materials gathered from your surroundings. Lulu will guide participants through two hours of playful exploration, learning, and good chats, perfect for anyone keen to try something new and make art in a relaxed, supportive environment.

5b4464df305add17.summary: Professor Jennifer Miles-Chan delivers her Inaugural Lecture exploring lessons found at the intersection of metabolism, academia, and everyday life.
5b4464df305add17.description: The lecture explores the interconnectedness of metabolism, academia, and life itself. Professor Miles-Chan draws insights from the biological processes of metabolism to offer new perspectives on academic practices and the nuances of daily existence. Attendees will gain a broader understanding of how fundamental biological concepts can illuminate broader human and societal experiences.

5e031be2b6ffbc07.summary: Explore the art of Bengali embroidery in a hands-on, communal session led by textile and community artist Sahana Rahman.

speakers.name: Sahana Rahman
speakers.bio: Textile and community artist specializing in Bengali embroidery.

600ca550291d52a4.summary: Professor Nicola Daly examines how picturebooks reflect societal values and what parents wish to pass on to the next generation.
600ca550291d52a4.description: Picturebooks are more than mere entertainment; they are powerful cultural artifacts. They reveal what generations of people value and what they hope to transmit to their mokopuna. This presentation explores the significance of these narratives in shaping cultural understanding.

61b44da4b6ece3fd.summary: Join us this Archaeology Week for a family-friendly event offering a unique opportunity to become an archaeologist for the day! Explore the fascinating world of uncovering history.
61b44da4b6ece3fd.description: Join us this Archaeology Week for a family-friendly event offering a unique opportunity to become an archaeologist for the day! Explore the fascinating world of uncovering history.

62204f2d9234daaf.summary: Mandarin Corner workshop on Chinese gold-foil burning art.
62204f2d9234daaf.description: Relaxed meet-up to practice Mandarin with native speakers. Explore the use of gold in Chinese art and create a simple gold-foil design.

62d42dd48958cee6.description: Hackathons offer a platform for students to engage in creative problem-solving with real-world applications. An AUT event for those interested in technology, innovation, and collaborative challenges.

6866a93602bd03ca.summary: Experience Day is about getting a taste of the experience of being a student at Massey University Wellington's College of Creative Arts - Toi Rauwhārangi.
6866a93602bd03ca.description: Experience Day is about getting a taste of the experience of being a student at Massey University Wellington's College of Creative Arts - Toi Rauwhārangi. Join us from 9:30am - 3pm on June 12th for workshops, pizza, student panel talks and more! (Registration will open from 9am) Experience days are free to attend, but registration is essential to secure your spot on your chosen workshop. Please note, you can only attend one workshop on the day, so choose the one you are most excited about!
6866a93602bd03ca.exclude_reason: Festival, not a lecture or academic talk component

69655c25a9a89e49.summary: Examine the mechanics and significance of voting and elections within the political structure of ancient Rome.
69655c25a9a89e49.description: This seminar explores the complex systems of voting and elections that governed the Republic and Empire of ancient Rome. Participants will gain insight into the political structures that allowed for citizen participation and democratic procedure in the classical world. Understanding these methods illuminates the foundations of Western political thought.

6a637e4eaaedf817.summary: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific.
6a637e4eaaedf817.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. Now a recognised regional arts event, the Festival presents its signature programme including the country’s largest print fair, live printing, artist talks and hands-on workshops. New for 2026 are curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours and experimental performances that expand how print is experienced. Taking place across four central Auckland venues, Printopia invites audiences to connect, explore and be inspired.
6a637e4eaaedf817.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or talk component

6c5fc1c057bcfa78.summary: Authors discuss writing from the margins and global publishing.
6c5fc1c057bcfa78.description: A conversation with three New Zealand authors exploring themes of writing from the margins, finding readership, and telling Kiwi stories in a globalised publishing market.

6c85bd14c67bf65d.summary: Neuroscientist Emeritus Distinguished Professor Cliff Abraham will discuss the process of memory formation and forgetting at the nerve cell level in the brain.
6c85bd14c67bf65d.description: Neuroscientist Emeritus Distinguished Professor Cliff Abraham will talk about what happens to the nerve cells in our brains to allow us to remember – or forget.

6cf49b2e5a77f75c.summary: AUT Worldwide is back!!!Mark your calendars – AUT Worldwide is taking over our campuses from 5 to 7 May. With students from nearly 140 countries, AUT is h
6cf49b2e5a77f75c.description: AUT Worldwide is back!!!Mark your calendars – AUT Worldwide is taking over our campuses from 5 to 7 May. With students from nearly 140 countries, AUT is h

6edac8ffc7e3fbf6.summary: Royal Society presents an urgent update on climate change.
6edac8ffc7e3fbf6.description: Join the Royal Society for a critical lecture addressing recent developments in climate science and their implications. Thin Ice 2026 aims to provide an urgent update on our planet's changing climate, highlighting key challenges and potential solutions.

6f04d15e54116813.summary: Doctoral candidates from the Faculties of Business and Economics and Law compete in the Three Minute Thesis Heat to showcase their research.
6f04d15e54116813.description: Doctoral candidates from the Faculty of Business and Economics and the Faculty of Law are invited to compete in the joint Three Minute Thesis (3MT) Heat. This competition challenges participants to present their entire thesis in just 180 seconds, offering a chance to showcase their research in a dynamic, compelling way and earn a place in the University 3MT Final.

6f8154369b41962d.summary: Professor Clare McCann delivers her Inaugural Lecture exploring the persuasive capabilities of speech-language therapists.
6f8154369b41962d.description: This inaugural lecture explores the art and science of persuasion as practiced by speech-language therapists. The talk examines the methods and techniques used to guide language development and behavioral change through communication. Audience members will gain insight into the power of language manipulation within therapeutic settings.

71fe220011968c3a.exclude_reason: Administrative application process, not academic talk.

7215d531111a9854.summary: The New Zealand Institute of International Affairs will hold its Annual General Meeting followed by an annual lecture featuring the Minister for Trade and Investment, Hon Todd McClay.
7215d531111a9854.description: The New Zealand Institute of International Affairs will hold its national Annual General Meeting, including an address by the NZIIA Chair, Dr James Kember. This is followed by the annual lecture at the Grand Hall, Parliament Buildings. The Minister for Trade and Investment, Hon Todd McClay, will present at the lecture.

730748b019323c6f.summary: Ara Poutama Aotearoa (Department of Corrections) presents research on using COBRA database data and GIS mapping to better understand and reduce prison violence.
730748b019323c6f.description: Ara Poutama Aotearoa (Department of Corrections) tracks every violent incident in prison through the COBRA database. This research looks at how that data can be combined with mapping tech (GIS) to better understand and help reduce prison violence.

7423082faeba0062.clean_title: Vibe coding or tech debt? Building fast without paying later
7423082faeba0062.summary: Discussing the balance between speed of development and long-term technical sustainability, 'Vibe coding or tech debt?' explores building fast without incurring future costs.
7423082faeba0062.description: Deloitte presents: Vibe coding or tech debt? Building fast without paying later examines the challenge of rapid development versus sustainable technical architecture. The event will take place at Deloitte Auckland on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

7458408263065f34.summary: The talk explores professors' involvement in the creation of the CIA following the U.S. fear of strategic surprise after Pearl Harbor.
7458408263065f34.description: The Cold War, the Bomb, and the Professors Who Turned around CIA examines the role of academics in intelligence history. The presentation explores the origins of the CIA, tracing its roots to the pervasive fear of strategic surprise established after the attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941. Attendees will gain insight into the intellectual currents shaping American intelligence agencies.

77b0a203c71c3323.summary: Canada's High Commissioner discusses Indigenous rights and UNDRIP.
77b0a203c71c3323.description: This meeting will start with an AGM followed by the High Commissioner’s talk on Canada’s approach to Indigenous rights, drawing from his personal and professional experience. The discussion will cover domestic commitments in a broader international context, focusing on cooperation between Indigenous peoples and governments, with special attention to synergies with Aotearoa New Zealand.

7879b554665913d7.summary: Screening of 'Welfare' (1975) with introduction and discussion on Aotearoa's welfare systems.
7879b554665913d7.description: This event features a screening of the film 'Welfare' from 1975, accompanied by an introductory talk and post-screening discussion led by artist Rangi White. The focus is on exploring and critiquing the bureaucratic structures within New Zealand's welfare system.

78c60ae414094338.summary: A quiz event at the Aotearoa Art Fair challenging participants' knowledge of art. The event is hosted by ArtSpace.

7bf84ac607a16c8c.clean_title: Regulatory Law through the Lens of Law-in-Context
7bf84ac607a16c8c.summary: Dr Simon Robb will explore law-in-context insights, drawing from his regulatory career, to deepen understanding of governance and regulatory practice.
7bf84ac607a16c8c.description: Dr Simon Robb, whose academic foundations lie in sociology and law, draws on his regulatory career to explore law-in-context insights. The presentation aims to deepen the understanding of governance and regulatory practice within the legal sphere.

7c69365a7120b66f.summary: Dr Oliver Hartwich discusses adapting to a tribal world in crisis.
7c69365a7120b66f.description: In today's complex global landscape marked by five interconnected crises—erosion of trust, decay at the institutional core, complacency about sustaining conditions, retreat from reason, and loss of Western confidence—Dr Oliver Hartwich explores adaptation strategies. He highlights these challenges as part of our current reality rather than future forecasts. Dr Hartwich is Executive Director of The New Zealand Initiative in Wellington.

7c6f453987ef3c34.summary: The first screening of the film 'fugue notes' (2025) by Selina Ershadi, remastered for the London BFI Film Festival.
7c6f453987ef3c34.description: This event features the first screening of 'fugue notes' (2025) by Selina Ershadi, which has been remastered for its inclusion in the London BFI Film Festival. The film was part of the Anatomy of Place, Sites of Becoming section of the festival.

7ee117204ef80cbf.clean_title: Love is an action: A zine-making workshop
7ee117204ef80cbf.summary: This zine-making workshop explores whakapapa, whanaungatanga, kinship, and community through DIY graphic design and collage, led by Kerry Ann Lee and Moira Clunie.
7ee117204ef80cbf.description: The workshop explores whakapapa, whanaungatanga, kinship, histories, mysteries, futures, friendships, and affinities through zine-making. Led by Kerry Ann Lee and Moira Clunie, the session explores playing with type, text, symbols, and images, and the role of zines in community building. Participants will use DIY graphic design, collage, zine-making, and letterpress printing to craft graphic gestures of love, hate, solidarity, care, and support in print.

speakers.name: Kerry Ann Lee
speakers.bio: Associate Professor in Design, Toi Rauwhārangi

speakers.name: Moira Clunie
speakers.bio: zinester, letterpress printer, social change practitioner

7fd7ba297f4c93f3.summary: Details For many generations, Aotearoa has invested comparatively little, certainly by OECD standards, in research and development (R and D). Moreover, since 2020–21,...

80a8f6a8a9efa1b2.summary: CreativeMornings Auckland returns after a reset period with a new organizing team, fresh themes, and inspiring speakers.

82e172e0631b1028.summary: Join a free, educational deep dive into Aotearoa New Zealand’s founding document, Te Tiriti o Waitangi.
82e172e0631b1028.description: To mark the signing of Te Tiriti o Waitangi (The Raukawa Moana sheet) on 29 April 1840, join us for a free, educational, deep dive into Aotearoa New Zealand’s founding document.

844b9ea53dc84eb6.summary: Mandarin Corner celebrates Dragon Boat Festival, crafts herbal sachets.
844b9ea53dc84eb6.description: Join our Mandarin Corner for a relaxed meet-up to practise Mandarin with native speakers. Celebrate the Dragon Boat Festival and craft your own traditional herbal sachet.

86b784eecf036925.summary: This talk explores New Zealand's growing space sector, the science behind domestic launch capabilities, and the policy frameworks for space use.
86b784eecf036925.description: This session explores New Zealand's rapidly growing space sector, the science enabled by domestic launch capability, and the policy frameworks governing our use of space.

87635656d44c1448.summary: Discuss the latest in AI-assisted coding at the Prompt Poets Society gathering at 2degrees headquarters.
87635656d44c1448.description: The cogs of AI progress keep a turning! Join the April 2026 Prompt Poets Society meeting at the 2degrees headquarters on Fanshawe St on April 28th at 6:00pm. Attendees will discuss and swap notes on AI-assisted coding in a collegial atmosphere, featuring prepared talks and open discussion. Pizza from Totos and beverages are provided.

87d8cabb11424c41.summary: Experience Chinese shadow puppetry at Victoria University.
87d8cabb11424c41.description: Experience the storytelling magic of traditional Chinese shadow puppetry. Watch a live demonstration, learn about its history and cultural significance.

8b30fa94d015ef93.summary: Film screening of Welfare (1975) with Rangi White's introduction on Aotearoa's welfare bureaucracies.
8b30fa94d015ef93.description: Artist Rangi White introduces a film screening of 'Welfare' (1975), directed by Frederick Wiseman, which explores the complexities and challenges within New Zealand's welfare system. The documentary highlights issues such as housing, unemployment, and medical problems faced by both clients and workers navigating bureaucratic laws.

90ea9f9ec069e662.summary: Prof. Dr. Martin Kment discusses AI's role in government decision-making, followed by a panel discussion with Paula Riano and Pedro Ramirez.
90ea9f9ec069e662.description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping how governments operate, raising profound questions about efficiency, accountability, and the future of public decision-making. Prof. Dr. Martin Kment will discuss AI and its emerging role in government decision-making processes, drawing on international developments, particularly in Europe. He will address whether and to what extent we should permit AI to make decisions traditionally reserved for human authorities. Following the keynote, a panel discussion will feature Paula Riano and Pedro Ramirez, concluding with a Q&A session.

speakers.name: Prof. Dr. Martin Kment
speakers.bio: Managing Director, Institute for Environmental Law, University of Augsburg

speakers.name: Paula Riano
speakers.bio: AI Strategy Advisor at Five New Zealand Consultancy

speakers.name: Pedro Ramirez
speakers.bio: Digital Strategy Practitioner – AI Work Programme, Department of Internal Affairs

93d3306be97bd05b.clean_title: Your next customer may not be human!
93d3306be97bd05b.summary: The event discusses the possibility that future customers may be non-human, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.
93d3306be97bd05b.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents 'Your next customer may not be human!'. This discussion explores the future of customer interaction, suggesting that the next generation of customers may not be human in nature. The event takes place at Deloitte Auckland on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

95282080ccc6256f.summary: Former diplomat Frank Wilson speaks about his experiences witnessing the Fall of Saigon in 1975 and its implications for New Zealand.
95282080ccc6256f.description: Frank Wilson, a former senior New Zealand diplomat with a strong career background in Asia, will discuss his experiences witnessing the Fall of Saigon in April 1975. He will cover his own accounts, those of other New Zealanders present, and the event's broader implications for New Zealand and the region.

95802a3f9566c770.summary: The lecture examines Shakespeare's role as a reviser, focusing specifically on 'The Merry Wives of Windsor.'
95802a3f9566c770.description: This lecture examines Shakespeare's craft as a reviser through the lens of 'The Merry Wives of Windsor.' It delves into how Shakespeare adapted and reinterpreted existing material to shape his canon. Attendees will gain insight into the processes of literary revision and the impact of such revisions on dramatic interpretation.

960eb2c54daf54c8.summary: Content Forecast: Blood, violence, depictions of mental distress and death. R13.About the showHamlet’s looking paler than usual. Their father’s dead, mother’s remarried, and...
960eb2c54daf54c8.description: Content Forecast: Blood, violence, depictions of mental distress and death. R13.About the showHamlet’s looking paler than usual. Their father’s dead, mother’s remarried, and...

speakers.name: THEATRE 318 (Te Kāhui: Company) and 319 (Scenography in Produ

968071dd09d57105.summary: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition with expanded programmes.
968071dd09d57105.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. The Festival presents a signature programme including the largest print fair in the country, live printing, artist talks, and hands-on workshops. New additions for 2026 include curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours, and experimental performances.

97b507e8a2192a3a.summary: Professor Prasanna Gai explores shipping lanes, inflation risk, and optimal monetary policy.
97b507e8a2192a3a.description: Professor Prasanna Gai delves into the relationship between global shipping lanes and economic instability, focusing on hub shocks and their implications for monetary policy. This lecture is part of the Treasury's guest lecture series, featuring expert analysis from leading economists.

9c3b2c04d77a480f.clean_title: Shaping Aotearoa New Zealand—The revolutionaries who reworked a nation and its culture
9c3b2c04d77a480f.summary: Colin James discusses the revolutionaries who reworked a national and its culture as part of the Stout Centre’s seminar series.
9c3b2c04d77a480f.description: This seminar from the Stout Centre’s seminar series highlights recent books in New Zealand Studies, concluding with Colin James’ discussion on the revolutionaries who reworked a national and its culture.

9c702e965742d928.summary: The final webinar in the 'This Thinking Life' series examines the historical dimensions of sound.
9c702e965742d928.description: This webinar completes the 'This Thinking Life' series, focusing on the historical aspects of sound. Participants will gain an understanding of how the perception and use of sound have evolved throughout history.

a0102426b2daac5e.summary: Professor Rae Frances Irvine addresses breakthrough research into the neuroscience of chronic pain and emerging treatments.
a0102426b2daac5e.description: The neuroscience of chronic pain remains a complex and evolving field. This lecture explores breakthrough research detailing how the brain processes pain signals and investigates the cutting-edge treatments emerging to manage chronic pain conditions. Attendees will gain insight into the latest understanding of pain's biological underpinnings.

a183aefe530f57d3.summary: Designer and artist Kerry Ann Lee will present a talk exploring creative whakapapa across time, people, and places in her printmaking work.
a183aefe530f57d3.description: This talk explores the creative whakapapa across time, people and places located at the heart of Lee’s work, which spans years of research. Focusing on ornament, printmaking, and ephemera, Lee will present a narrative that reveals emotional landscapes living in the space between the studio and the pull of wanderlust into the field. Lee is a visual artist, designer and researcher whose interdisciplinary practice spans art, design and publishing.

speakers.name: Kerry Ann Lee
speakers.bio: Associate Professor in Design, Massey University

a244e0dfe41b06b5.summary: Ruth Buchanan guides attendees through the 'The Blue Dome' exhibition, offering insight into Selina Ersahdi’s newly commissioned films and installations.
a244e0dfe41b06b5.description: Ruth Buchanan will guide attendees through the exhibition 'The Blue Dome,' providing insight into the newly commissioned films and installations by artist Selina Ersahdi. Ruth will discuss the development of the films, inviting audiences to learn more about the artistic process.

a2e7b0f7f3d197cd.summary: Ceremony marking the conclusion of teaching programmes.
a2e7b0f7f3d197cd.description: This ceremony marks the completion of Victoria University's Graduate Diploma of Teaching and Master of Teaching and Learning programmes, celebrating the achievements of graduating students.

a38c1adb5dea7997.summary: A full day event for podcast producers, presenters, practitioners, providers, and the podcast curious across Aotearoa.
a38c1adb5dea7997.description: This event is for podcast producers, presenters, practitioners, providers from across Aotearoa, and those just curious about podcasts. It is a full day dedicated to the podcasting industry.

a616ebba62bce37b.summary: Webinar discussing NZ's $12B defence commitment and modernisation needs, featuring Major General John Howard and Sir Rod Drury.
a616ebba62bce37b.description: New Zealand has committed $12 billion to defence over four years, with a path to spending more than 2% of GDP within eight years. This webinar launches research on the need for institutional reforms beyond financial investment in modernizing defense capabilities. Speakers will discuss strategic areas like intelligence, space, domestic innovation and digital architecture.

a70971ae735940bb.summary: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore.
a70971ae735940bb.description: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore. Historically a trading language that linked people across maritime Southeast Asia, contemporary Indonesian is relatively easy to pick up for basic communication purposes, whether you’re planning a holiday in Bali or a business trip to Jakarta.
a70971ae735940bb.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or talk component.

a73df608b35aec92.summary: A conversation with Rotuman artists Sofia Tekela-Smith and Maluseu Monise celebrating Rotuman Language Week.
a73df608b35aec92.description: This event, Kạp‘ạki - To Embrace, is a conversation with Rotuman artists Sofia Tekela-Smith and Maluseu Monise to celebrate Rotuman Language Week.

a7e028a3570d6069.summary: This workshop introduces participants to the world of Chinese martial arts through guided movements, cultural insights, and educational sessions.

a99209c8cf951dc1.summary: Lisa Beauchamp and Ruth Minh Ha will lead a guided tour of Studies for a Keepsake: Claude Cahun and Marcel Moore, discussing the exhibition's curatorial framework.
a99209c8cf951dc1.description: Lisa Beauchamp, Curator of Contemporary Art, and Ruth Minh Ha, Assistant Curator, will lead a guided tour of the exhibition Studies for a Keepsake: Claude Cahun and Marcel Moore. They will speak to the curatorial framework that shaped the first Aotearoa exhibition of these pioneering artists.

aba91cecd08be353.summary: Victoria University hosts linguistics seminar on arguing about morphemes.
aba91cecd08be353.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars, featuring a selection of visiting researchers and local experts. This week's topic explores the nuances and debates surrounding morphemes in linguistic theory.

speakers.name: Prof. Laurie Bauer
speakers.bio: School of Linguistics and Applied Language

ac7d494ba6b6c5c1.summary: The seventh webinar in the 'This Thinking Life' series explores challenging patriarchy by reassessing history through the lens of ancient women.
ac7d494ba6b6c5c1.description: This session tackles the complex structure of patriarchy by reexamining historical narratives through the experiences of ancient women. Participants will gain a critical lens to evaluate established gender roles and societal power structures across different civilizations. The discussion aims to foster a deeper understanding of female agency outside conventional historical frameworks.

acb9de0bf9304fb7.clean_title: Human × machine lab: Getting human and machine relationships right in HR and workforce decisions
acb9de0bf9304fb7.summary: This session explores how organizations can effectively redefine HR responsibilities by managing the growing relationships between humans and machines in workforce decisions.
acb9de0bf9304fb7.description: The intersection of human and machine capabilities is fundamentally reshaping Human Resources responsibilities. This session examines the critical challenge of establishing the right operational and ethical relationships between human employees and technological systems. Attendees will gain insights into modernizing HR processes to effectively manage a blended workforce model.

acc08f44e4d1fa88.exclude_reason: General exhibition event, not a lecture.

adb78f3c44febf5d.summary: Join Dr Pilar Villamor for the annual Hochstetter Lecture, as she reviews 25 years of trenching active faults in volcanic environments and how a scientist's perspective on tectonics close to volcanoes changed through that time.
adb78f3c44febf5d.description: Join Dr Pilar Villamor for the annual Hochstetter Lecture, as she reviews 25 years of trenching active faults in volcanic environments and how a scientist's perspective on tectonics close to volcanoes changed through that time.

ae1b385a7cc1d662.summary: This event hosts the annual Sir John Graham Lecture, providing insights into a specific topic related to the institute's focus.
ae1b385a7cc1d662.description: The Sir John Graham Lecture serves as a platform for deep academic discussion on key intellectual themes relevant to the institute's work. Attendees will gain expert perspectives on the featured subject matter through this annual, specialized address.

aeb312a738e9e76d.summary: This session explores a hybrid battery swapping-charging system designed to manage the pressure of expanding EV battery-swapping networks on urban power grids.
aeb312a738e9e76d.description: The rapid expansion of electric vehicle battery-swapping networks is increasing pressure on urban power grids. The study examines a hybrid battery swapping-charging system featuring multiple local swapping stations (SSs) and one central charging station (CS). Depleted batteries are managed either by charging at SSs or by transport to the CS for centralized charging and redistribution.

af0a10e5eabd00ca.summary: Professor Derick de Jongh will examine the distinctive contours of African versus Western leadership theories, using the case of Chief Albert Luthuli.
af0a10e5eabd00ca.description: Professor Derick de Jongh will highlight a prominent leadership example from the case of Chief Albert Luthuli, examining the distinctive contours of African versus Western leadership theories.

af83032b7b0af944.summary: Former Asia-Pacific Editor of the Washington Post, Anna Fifield, will discuss her career as a foreign correspondent for international newspapers.
af83032b7b0af944.description: Anna Fifield was the Asia-Pacific Editor for the Washington Post until February this year, and previously edited the Dominion Post in Wellington. She spent 20 years as a foreign correspondent for publications including the Financial Times and the Washington Post across postings in Seoul, Tehran, Beirut, Washington DC, Tokyo, and Beijing. A Nieman journalism fellow at Harvard University, she received the Stanford University Shorenstein Journalism Award in 2018 for her Asian reporting. Her book, "The Great Successor: The Secret Rise and Rule of Kim Jong-Un," is translated into 24 languages.

speakers.name: Anna Fifield
speakers.bio: Former Asia-Pacific Editor of the Washington Post

b00b8f45e8db70bf.summary: A panel discussion featuring fiction finalists explores the themes, craft, and challenges inherent in writing contemporary New Zealand fiction.
b00b8f45e8db70bf.description: This discussion illuminates the current landscape of New Zealand fiction. Attendees will gain insight into the thematic currents, technical craftsmanship, and creative challenges confronting contemporary authors. The conversation provides a deep look into the state and future direction of the nation's literary output.

b06dbc473ad56b9a.summary: Come join us for a free 45-minute taster class to learn some basics. Learn how to greet, introduce yourself and use some everyday...
b06dbc473ad56b9a.description: Come join us for a free 45-minute taster class to learn some basics. Learn how to greet, introduce yourself and use some everyday...

b3e2988035940c65.summary: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring visiting researchers like Dr Mahnaz Aliyar.
b3e2988035940c65.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the. This seminar is presented by Dr Mahnaz Aliyar, LALS, Victoria University of Wellington.

b57944a3b4ba7563.summary: PhD candidate Hirdey Rampal discusses how people sustain well-being in the context of Spiti Valley, India.
b57944a3b4ba7563.description: Hirdey Rampal, a PhD candidate in Cultural Anthropology at Te Herenga Waka – Victoria University of Wellington, explores how people sustain well-being when navigating change in Spiti Valley, India.

speakers.name: Hirdey Rampal
speakers.bio: PhD candidate in Cultural Anthropology at Te Herenga Waka – Victoria University of Wellington

ba2dd526b346cb10.clean_title: The Art of the Possible: Leveraging AI for Supply Chain Resilience
ba2dd526b346cb10.summary: This talk explores how artificial intelligence can be leveraged to build resilience within modern supply chains.
ba2dd526b346cb10.description: The integration of artificial intelligence represents a transformative opportunity for enhancing supply chain resilience. Attendees will examine practical methods for leveraging AI to anticipate disruptions and build more robust operational networks. This discussion offers insights into keeping complex supply chains adaptable in a volatile global environment.

bc36ce59b7e81181.description: Public Record workshop at our gallery space     Kintsugi (Modernised - Mending method / Art form) Kintsugi (also known as Kintsukuroi) which loosely translates to 'Golden Joinery' is the age-old Japanese art form of repairing broken ceramics in a way that is often considered more beautiful than when it was when whole.
bc36ce59b7e81181.exclude_reason: It is an art/craft workshop, not academic.

bca72501b20fb8eb.summary: Learn about choosing postgraduate programmes, career pathways, and exploring research interests at this online webinar.
bca72501b20fb8eb.description: Learn about choosing programmes, career pathways, and exploring your research interests during this online postgraduate webinar.

be71cfa05e7f3e78.summary: A practical nutrition workshop designed to help Auckland University students maintain a healthy diet while studying on campus.

c1776f477373865c.summary: Artist Rangi White introduces a film screening of Frederick Wiseman's Welfare, exploring Aotearoa’s welfare bureaucracies.
c1776f477373865c.description: Artist Rangi White draws on the aesthetics of a Work and Income Office to consider how material and spatial conditions structure a person’s encounters with the state. The event includes a film screening of 'Welfare (1975)' directed by Frederick Wiseman, which explores the complexity of the welfare system.

c18c3e744bc93c82.summary: Examines the history of Redcoat soldiers stationed in New Zealand and throughout the British Empire.
c18c3e744bc93c82.description: This seminar delves into the history of Redcoat soldiers across the British Empire, focusing specifically on their presence in New Zealand. Understanding the experiences of these troops reveals deeper insights into colonial administration and military life in the Pacific region. The discussion provides a focused look at archival research concerning these historical military populations.

c286231c9816d9f2.summary: This session explores Indigenous psychologies, psychology, and the relationship with Te Tiriti in the 'This Thinking Life' series.
c286231c9816d9f2.description: This second webinar in the 'This Thinking Life' series examines the intersections between Indigenous psychologies, established psychological frameworks, and the principles of Te Tiriti. The discussion centers on how these different knowledge systems interact within modern psychological practice and understanding.

c2fc63ea6435fa7c.summary: Matthew Hanson will guide attendees through the Artspace Aotearoa championed artists and artworks at the Aotearoa Art Fair.
c2fc63ea6435fa7c.description: Matthew Hanson, Kaiāwhina Whakaaturanga Assistant Curator, will lead an exploration of the artists and artworks featured at the Aotearoa Art Fair that Artspace Aotearoa has supported through its decades of collaboration. Attendees should arrive at 11:15am to receive their headset.

c33d0f15166dfb58.summary: This talk will deep dive into the history, significant figures, and contemporary developments in botanical art and illustration.
c33d0f15166dfb58.description: This talk will deep dive into the history, significant figures, and contemporary developments in botanical art and illustration.

c3fe748234d3e3ec.summary: A free interactive drop-in session for tamariki aged 5 and under. This month learn about sharing stories in New Zealand Sign language.
c3fe748234d3e3ec.description: A free interactive drop-in session for tamariki aged 5 and under. This month learn about sharing stories in New Zealand Sign language.
c3fe748234d3e3ec.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or talk component

c6c4b309d0526a61.summary: In celebration of New Zealand Music Month, join Auckland Youth Choir for two special performances in two of the Museum's most iconic spaces: Te Ao Mārama South Atrium, accompanied by the rich tones of the Museum’s Fazioli grand piano, and the stunning acoustics of the Grand Foyer.
c6c4b309d0526a61.description: In celebration of New Zealand Music Month, join Auckland Youth Choir for two special performances in two of the Museum's most iconic spaces: Te Ao Mārama South Atrium, accompanied by the rich tones of the Museum’s Fazioli grand piano, and the stunning acoustics of the Grand Foyer.

c918880121cd1fe6.summary: Lawrence explores how Europe and New Zealand are strengthening ties in an increasingly interconnected and contested global environment.
c918880121cd1fe6.description: Lawrence will explore how Europe and New Zealand are strengthening ties in an increasingly interconnected and contested global environment. His remarks will highlight the value of working with like-minded partners in the Asia-Pacific region to uphold open, rules-based cooperation and deliver practical outcomes.

ca1f64f253399828.summary: A special film screening celebrating New Zealand Sign Language week, exploring the visual language of the country.
ca1f64f253399828.description: The screening showcases 'Sign of the Times – The Story of New Zealand’s Visual Language.' This film offers an in-depth look at the rich visual culture and evolving narrative embedded within New Zealand Sign Language, celebrating the unique linguistic identity.

cbe18b8db8f4d84b.summary: Mike Seawright discusses over two decades of delivering humanitarian assistance to families in the world’s conflict zones.
cbe18b8db8f4d84b.description: Mike Seawright has spent over two decades delivering humanitarian assistance to families in conflict zones worldwide. He has provided aid in Iraq, Yemen, Sudan, and Pakistan, and recently led interventions in Syria, Afghanistan, and Ukraine. Frustrated by insufficient aid, he founded ReliefAid to help families with no recourse. ReliefAid has provided life-saving aid to over 1.6 million people in areas like Gaza, Syria, Lebanon, and Ukraine.

cce7967c19d96aa3.clean_title: Regional tech panel: Future of tech in Marlborough
cce7967c19d96aa3.summary: A panel discussion hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 focusing on the future of technology within the Marlborough region.
cce7967c19d96aa3.description: This panel discussion, presented by Deloitte Techweek26, explores the future of technology in Marlborough. Attendees can find more event information at Te Au Pūngao, Level 1/33 Arthur Street, Blenheim Central, Blenheim 7201, New Zealand on Monday, May 18, 2026.

ce1f249b3c34b63f.summary: This workshop centers the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, exploring how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, intersecting with gender and identity.
ce1f249b3c34b63f.description: This workshop centers the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence. Participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body through discussion, drawing, writing, and embodied practice. The session invites critical reflection on cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems, grounded in Rosanna Raymond's own malu and artistic practice.

ced2707cbf21f28a.clean_title: Agentic blueprinting: Driving value from AI
ced2707cbf21f28a.summary: Deloitte presents a talk on agentic blueprinting, focusing on driving value from Artificial Intelligence.
ced2707cbf21f28a.description: Deloitte presents Agentic blueprinting: Driving value from AI. This event is hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 and takes place at Deloitte Wellington in Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

cee11c9c166f76f7.summary: Explore the world of sharks from prehistoric times to today in a theatrical journey. Dive into deep waters without the need for a wetsuit or breathing apparatus.

d877bcff9e529644.summary: Elizabeth Cox discusses Mr Ward's Map as part of the Stout Centre's seminar series highlighting recent New Zealand studies books.
d877bcff9e529644.description: The Stout Centre’s seminar series, Between the Lines—Recent Books in New Zealand Studies, continues with Elizabeth Cox discussing Mr Ward's Map. This session highlights recent scholarly publications concerning New Zealand history and culture.

d92b21fc2b42ebf3.summary: International Research Symposium on work-integrated learning.
d92b21fc2b42ebf3.description: The International Research Symposium, organized by WACE, explores transforming practice through research in work-integrated education. This event aims to bring together global experts for a collaborative dialogue and innovation in cooperative and experiential educational methods.

dab41faa544e7e75.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 examines demographics, productivity, and the impact of AI choices facing New Zealand.
dab41faa544e7e75.description: This talk, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26, will explore crucial topics facing New Zealand, specifically focusing on demographics, productivity challenges, and the implications of artificial intelligence.

db21f3bf1d770c7f.summary: Exploring societal challenges in New Zealand.
db21f3bf1d770c7f.description: In Aotearoa, key issues like poverty, inequity, ecological destruction, and climate change are well understood but require urgent action. This lecture delves into these problems and potential solutions for a just society.

speakers.name: Boyd Scirkovich
speakers.bio: Chief Economic Development and Investment Officer

speakers.name: Gareth Hughes
speakers.bio: Director Wellbeing Economy Alliance Aotearoa NZ

speakers.name: Bianca Elkington
speakers.bio: General Manager Education and Employment, Te

speakers.name: Fleur Fitzsimons
speakers.bio: National Secretary Public Service Association Te

speakers.name: Edward Miller
speakers.bio: Researcher, Centre for International Corporate Tax

speakers.name: Rebecca Kiddle and James Berghan
speakers.bio: Director and senior research fellow at

e1cb94aaa2fee747.summary: Victoria hosts a lecture on philanthropy in supply chains.
e1cb94aaa2fee747.description: Victoria University presents 'Philanthropy Along the Supply Chain' by authors Huu Nhan Duong, Emdad Islam, Lubna Rahman, and Cara Vansteenkiste. Abstract focuses on strategic use of donations to trade partners’ affiliated charities.

e2788c45a4a1bec8.summary: UC engineers reflect on lessons learned fifteen years after the 2011 Canterbury earthquakes, addressing changes in global building codes and remaining needs.
e2788c45a4a1bec8.description: Fifteen years after the 2011 Canterbury earthquakes, University of Canterbury engineers will reflect on what was learned, what has changed in global building codes, and what still needs to happen.

e32518eb218ec103.description: Discover how science and creativity intersect through cyanotyping. Experiment with UV light and natural materials to create unique prints under the guidance of Lulu, fostering a relaxed and supportive artistic environment.

e42257690cdde024.clean_title: Uncover the art of the possible: Deloitte x ServiceNow UC student appathon
e42257690cdde024.summary: Deloitte and ServiceNow, in partnership with the University of Canterbury, are hosting a Techweek Appathon challenge for UC students.
e42257690cdde024.description: This Techweek Appathon challenge brings together Deloitte, ServiceNow, and the University of Canterbury. Students will engage in a collaborative building exercise to explore and develop innovative applications using industry technology platforms.

e48ddbbc834dc652.summary: Marine scientists will share recent discoveries from deep-sea voyages to the Southern Ocean, covering new species and ecosystem dynamics.
e48ddbbc834dc652.description: Marine scientists will share discoveries from recent voyages to the Southern Ocean. Topics covered include newly identified species, ecosystem dynamics, and the implications for fisheries management.

e63364a32a4ba7ef.summary: Attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch AGM and hear Catherine Grant Makokera present on New Zealand’s Trade Policy Amid Global Conflict.
e63364a32a4ba7ef.description: Attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch’s Annual General Meeting for 2026 on Tuesday, April 28th, 2026. The AGM will cover the past year, thank the committee, outline upcoming events, and elect new committee members. Following the AGM, Catherine Grant Makokera will present on New Zealand’s Trade Policy During an Era of Geoeconomics and Global Conflict.

e7ee177f3796e430.summary: Explore the journey, history, and mātauranga surrounding the prestigious pauku cloak at Auckland Museum.
e7ee177f3796e430.description: The Auckland War Memorial Museum invites you to hear about the journey of the pauku, its history, care, and the important mātauranga surrounding this prestigious cloak. Join us for an evening at Tāmaki Paenga Hira.

e885f0a7f721ce37.summary: Researchers and community voices discuss the competing demands on Canterbury's freshwater resources and pathways to a sustainable future.
e885f0a7f721ce37.description: Researchers and community voices will discuss the competing demands placed on Canterbury's freshwater resources. The discussion aims to explore what a sustainable future for the region looks like.

e8cbee17043daa26.summary: Workshop on Siva Samoa movement and cultural significance.
e8cbee17043daa26.description: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where participants explore foundational steps and their cultural meanings. Through gentle movements, they experience the connection to nature and community, promoting health and collective wellbeing.

ea1ea9dc21d3e573.summary: This lecture examines the speed, volatility, and unpredictable nature of America under Donald Trump's potential second term in the Indo-Pacific.
ea1ea9dc21d3e573.description: Given the speed, volatility, and unpredictable nature of America during Donald Trump's second term as president, distinguishing between various global political signals can be challenging.

ec949af9f84b83e4.summary: Explore the future of fitness and discover the innovative world of sports technology through a weekend filled with engaging activities.

f3fabdf5c39be407.summary: Artist Selina Ershadi and Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan provide an informal introduction to The Blue Dome.
f3fabdf5c39be407.description: Alongside the Aotearoa Art Fair and neighbourhood gallery openings, the gallery will remain open until 8pm. Attendees can join artist Selina Ershadi and Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan for drinks and an informal introduction to The Blue Dome and its newly commissioned works.

f575220e54475c4f.summary: This event series reflects on the 50th anniversary of the Bachelor of Social Work qualification to inform the present and envision the future.
f575220e54475c4f.description: This event series celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Bachelor of Social Work qualification. The event reflects on the past while aiming to inform the present and envision the future of the field.

f736386a033bed4f.summary: Helen Howell presents research on visual priming and speech perception among Samoan speakers.
f736386a033bed4f.description: Helen Howell presents research on visual priming and speech perception among Samoan speakers. The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the.

f9522d3572d03fb2.summary: Future Environments Thought Leadership session, in partnership with Whaikaha – Ministry of Disabled People, focused on accessible and inclusive design in
f9522d3572d03fb2.description: Future Environments Thought Leadership session, in partnership with Whaikaha – Ministry of Disabled People, focused on accessible and inclusive design in

speakers.name: Rebecca Elvy
speakers.bio: Deputy Chief Executive Outreach and Innovation

f97d2adb031d11ec.summary: Mahdis Azarmandi presents a lecture on 'Inheriting Revolution: On Iran,' offering insight into the complex context of war and peace in Iran.
f97d2adb031d11ec.description: Drawing from her research within Peace and Conflict Studies, Mahdis Azarmandi will present a lecture on 'Inheriting Revolution: On Iran.' This talk provides insight into the complex context that plays a significant role in the region's history and future.

speakers.name: Mahdis Azarmandi
speakers.bio: Research within Peace and Conflict Studies

f97ff68eb8b1af43.summary: A deep dive discussion exploring the life and work of one of New Zealand’s finest early photographers, Leslie Adkin.
f97ff68eb8b1af43.description: This session provides an in-depth look at the life and significant contributions of Leslie Adkin, a pioneering figure in early New Zealand photography. Attendees will gain a deeper understanding of the photographic techniques and historical context that defined Adkin's work, revealing insights into the era's visual culture.

fdd4532b42c7f1b5.summary: Explore the Business Administration – Postgraduate Certificate in Business and its pathway to an Executive MBA.
fdd4532b42c7f1b5.description: This online info session explores the Business Administration – Postgraduate Certificate in Business and details how it functions as a pathway toward an Executive MBA.

fea2f6e6b34612a6.summary: Ruth Buchanan will present a live interpretation of her artwork and published collection Evacuation Tapes, exploring the construction of the self and the ways our bodies function.
fea2f6e6b34612a6.description: Kaitohu Director and artist Ruth Buchanan will present a live interpretation of her artwork and published collection Evacuation Tapes (2019). This spoken word work will explore the construction of the self and the ways in which our bodies function.

ff3963329dd882d8.summary: Professor Andrew A. Schwartz examines the concept of governance within the context of the fourth dimension.
ff3963329dd882d8.description: The talk explores the complex theoretical space of governance as it intersects with the concept of a fourth dimension. Attendees will gain insight into how established governance models must adapt to increasingly complex, multidimensional systems. This presentation delves into the mathematical and social structures underpinning future forms of governance.

ff8ae22d11fe7fac.summary: Artists Selina Ershadi and Nova Paul lead a workshop exploring film as a tool for telling personal stories for early career artists.
ff8ae22d11fe7fac.description: Artists and filmmakers Selina Ershadi and Nova Paul lead this one-day workshop for early career artists interested in moving image making. The workshop will draw on their interest in film as a key tool to tell personal stories.
